stories.title: Automatic Git Hooks Installation
stories.story: As a Rust developer, I want git hooks to be automatically installed when I run 'ferrous-forge safety install' so that I don't have to manually configure pre-commit and pre-push checks
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I run 'ferrous-forge safety install' in a git repository
WHEN the command completes successfully
THEN pre-commit and pre-push hooks should be installed in .git/hooks/
AND the hooks should run ferrous-forge validation checks
AND I should be able to uninstall them with 'ferrous-forge safety uninstall'
stories.businessValue: Reduces friction in adopting standards, prevents bad code from entering repository
stories.technicalNotes: Must handle existing hooks gracefully, support all major shells

stories.title: Documentation Coverage Enforcement
stories.story: As a team lead, I want to enforce minimum documentation coverage (80%) so that our codebase remains maintainable and new developers can understand the code
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I run 'ferrous-forge validate'
WHEN documentation coverage is below 80%
THEN the validation should warn about low coverage
AND show which modules need documentation
AND provide specific suggestions for improvement
stories.businessValue: Improves code maintainability, reduces onboarding time for new developers
stories.technicalNotes: Parse rustdoc output, calculate coverage percentage

stories.title: Test Coverage Integration
stories.story: As a QA engineer, I want test coverage to be automatically calculated and enforced so that I can ensure adequate test coverage without manual checks
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I have cargo-tarpaulin installed
WHEN I run 'ferrous-forge validate'
THEN test coverage should be calculated
AND coverage below minimum threshold should fail validation
AND a coverage report should be generated
AND coverage trends should be trackable
stories.businessValue: Ensures code quality, reduces bugs in production

stories.title: Embedded Project Template
stories.story: As an embedded developer, I want a template for embedded Rust projects so that I can quickly start a new microcontroller project with best practices
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I run 'ferrous-forge template create embedded my-project'
WHEN the command completes
THEN a no_std embedded project should be created
AND it should include memory.x configuration
AND it should have panic handler and entry point
AND it should be ready for common microcontrollers
stories.businessValue: Accelerates embedded development, ensures best practices from start

stories.title: Workspace Project Management
stories.story: As a software architect, I want a workspace template so that I can structure multi-crate projects with shared dependencies and coordinated versioning
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I run 'ferrous-forge template create workspace my-workspace'
WHEN the template is created
THEN a Cargo workspace should be set up
AND it should include 2-3 example member crates
AND shared dependencies should be managed at workspace level
AND workspace commands should work correctly
stories.businessValue: Enables proper monorepo structure, improves dependency management
stories.technicalNotes: Handle workspace Cargo.toml, member crate generation

stories.title: Plugin Development Support
stories.story: As a plugin developer, I want a template for creating plugins so that I can build extensions with proper API boundaries and loading mechanisms
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I run 'ferrous-forge template create plugin my-plugin'
WHEN the template is created
THEN a plugin project structure should be generated
AND it should include trait definitions for plugin API
AND it should have example host application
AND documentation for plugin development should be included

stories.title: WebAssembly Project Setup
stories.story: As a web developer, I want a WASM template so that I can create Rust code that runs in browsers with proper bindings and build configuration
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I run 'ferrous-forge template create wasm my-wasm-app'
WHEN the template is created
THEN a wasm-pack compatible project should be generated
AND it should include wasm-bindgen configuration
AND JavaScript bindings should be set up
AND example HTML file should demonstrate usage
stories.businessValue: Enables Rust in web development, improves performance-critical web apps

stories.title: Community Template Sharing
stories.story: As an open source contributor, I want to share and use community templates so that I can benefit from and contribute to collective best practices
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I have created a custom template
WHEN I run 'ferrous-forge template publish'
THEN my template should be validated and published
AND others should be able to fetch it by name
AND version management should be supported
AND templates should be validated before use
stories.businessValue: Builds community, shares best practices, accelerates development

stories.title: Cargo Publish Safety Gate
stories.story: As a package maintainer, I want cargo publish to run validation checks automatically so that I never accidentally publish code with violations to crates.io
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I have ferrous-forge installed with cargo intercept
WHEN I run 'cargo publish'
THEN ferrous-forge validation should run first
AND publication should be blocked if violations exist
AND I should see clear error messages about violations
AND there should be an emergency bypass option
stories.businessValue: Prevents publishing low-quality code, maintains crate ecosystem quality
stories.technicalNotes: Wrapper script for cargo, PATH manipulation

stories.title: Hierarchical Configuration
stories.story: As a DevOps engineer, I want hierarchical configuration so that I can set organization-wide standards while allowing project-specific overrides
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I have configs at system, user, and project levels
WHEN ferrous-forge runs
THEN it should merge configurations with proper precedence
AND project config should override user config
AND user config should override system config
AND I should be able to see effective configuration
stories.businessValue: Enables organization-wide standards, reduces configuration duplication

stories.title: Performance Optimization
stories.story: As a CI/CD engineer, I want validation to run quickly so that CI pipelines don't slow down development feedback loops
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN a large Rust project
WHEN I run 'ferrous-forge validate'
THEN validation should complete in under 2 seconds
AND memory usage should stay below 100MB
AND parallel processing should be utilized
AND results should be cached when appropriate
stories.businessValue: Faster CI pipelines, better developer experience
stories.technicalNotes: Rayon for parallelism, smart caching strategies

stories.title: Comprehensive Documentation
stories.story: As a new user, I want comprehensive documentation so that I can quickly learn how to use Ferrous Forge effectively in my projects
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I visit the documentation
WHEN I look for information about any feature
THEN I should find clear explanations
AND there should be practical examples
AND common issues should be addressed
AND migration guides should exist for version upgrades
stories.businessValue: Reduces support burden, increases adoption, improves user satisfaction
stories.technicalNotes: Markdown docs, API documentation, architecture diagrams

stories.title: Cross-Platform Testing
stories.story: As a Windows developer, I want Ferrous Forge to work seamlessly on Windows so that I can use the same quality standards as Linux/Mac developers
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN I'm on Windows
WHEN I use any Ferrous Forge feature
THEN it should work identically to Linux/Mac
AND file paths should be handled correctly
AND git hooks should work with Git for Windows
AND performance should be comparable
stories.businessValue: Enables adoption across all platforms, ensures consistency
stories.technicalNotes: Handle path separators, shell differences, line endings
